type: micro-SaaS / B2B tool (AI invoice collection & reconciliation)
asking_price: $5,000 (TrustMRR; 1.3x stated ≈ 1.25x annualized MRR — arithmetic honest, but on 5 subs / $660 all-time)
one_line_reason: The best-shaped target from TrustMRR so far — real B2B recurring revenue ($333 MRR, 5 subs, $29–79 tiers) in a category with a genuine EU e-invoicing regulatory tailwind — but 3 of its 5 showcase customers are the founder's own products, its stated growth channel is content/SEO the team can't run, and the $5,000 asking is 1.67x the budget with no financing.

operability_upside.note: Exact stack fit (Next.js/React/Supabase/Vercel/Cloudflare/Stripe). Real product upside: more integrations, more countries' e-invoicing, better matching. Drag: Italian tax compliance (autofattura XML, Stripe→fattura elettronica) and Italian-speaking accountant support are outside the team's domain.

pmf_retention.note: 5 paying subs at real B2B price points ($29–79 + add-ons) after ~5 months, +53%/30d. But the base is tiny, retention is unknowable, and the showcase customers (VideoTok, Editby, Filmia) are the founder's own products — the paying subs may be his network.

unit_economics.note: Recurring B2B at ~$66 ARPU, 85% margin claimed; AI extraction costs modest. Structurally sound if the customers are real and stay. Unverified.

moat_defensibility.note: Crowded, commoditizing category: Dext, Hubdoc (free with QBO), AutoEntry, Datamolino, GetMyInvoices, plus indie clones (Tailride, Taxing.app, Gennai). LLMs make extraction table-stakes. The Italy e-invoicing angle is a modest differentiator, but Italian incumbents (Fatture in Cloud, TeamSystem, Aruba) own that market.

growth_market.note: Genuine regulatory tailwind: EU e-invoicing mandates are expanding (Italy live, France and others rolling out), and SMB invoice automation is a real growing need. AI is both tailwind (product) and threat (commoditizes the core).

tech_ip.note: Modern owned stack (assumed). Two dependencies to verify: (1) Gmail access uses Google restricted scopes — the OAuth app needs Google verification (CASA security assessment), which may not transfer and carries recurring compliance cost; (2) SDI/e-invoicing likely goes through an Italian intermediary whose contract must transfer.

transfer_key_person.note: Distribution rides the founder: Product Hunt launches, his indie-hacker audience (X/Medium), his studio's cross-promotion, and content marketing. Showcase customers are largely his own companies. Much of the demand engine walks away with him.

deal_terms.note: 1.3x annualized MRR is below market for growing B2B SaaS on paper — but it's $5,000 for $660 of all-time revenue from 5 subscribers, and 1.67x the team's budget with no financing. 2 offers already received reduces negotiating room.

seller_adverse_selection.note: 'Shifting focus to a new venture' is credible serial-builder churn (4+ products at The Formula AI). But selling a 5-month-old product while its logos are his own companies, at a below-market multiple, says the insider doesn't value the future revenue — price the signal in.

key_unknowns: Who the 5 paying subscribers actually are — if 2–3 are the founder's own companies or friends, real external MRR may be ~$100–150 and likely churns on transfer; this is the single biggest swing (value_transfers gate + pmf).

Google OAuth restricted-scope verification status (gmail.readonly requires a CASA security assessment) — whether the verified OAuth app transfers to a new owner or requires re-verification, and at what recurring cost; a 'no' makes the product unusable for new Gmail users until re-verified.
The SDI / fattura elettronica integration path — which Italian intermediary is used, whether the contract transfers, and how much ongoing Italian compliance work the product demands.

Actual traffic and signup sources — stated channels are blog/content/SEO (DR 9) plus the founder's audience; if there is any product-led or integration-led acquisition it would flip growth_lever_fits, but nothing visible suggests it.
Churn to date — 5 months is short but any cancellations among early subs would confirm the network-revenue thesis.
Whether the seller would take ≤$3,000–3,750 — 2 offers are already in; the budget gate stands unless the price moves or financing appears.
seller_questions: List the 5 active subscriptions by company (or anonymized industry + plan + start date) and confirm none are owned by you, The Formula AI, or co-founders/friends. Share read-only Stripe access to reconcile. — resolves: value_transfers / pmf / revenue_evidence → buyer_verified; why: your showcase logos (VideoTok, Editby, Filmia) are your own products — if the subs are too, the revenue doesn't survive the sale.

What is the Google OAuth verification status for the Gmail restricted scopes — verified, in review, or under 100-user exemption? Does the verified Cloud project transfer with the sale, and what did the CASA assessment cost? — resolves: ip_transferable / not_fragile; why: without a transferable verified app, new Gmail connections break.

Which provider handles SDI / fattura elettronica and autofattura XML, under what contract and cost, and does it transfer? How much Italy-specific compliance maintenance has been needed in 5 months? — resolves: tech_ip / operability; why: the buyer team is not Italian and can't absorb a compliance treadmill.

Share the 90-day funnel: visitors by source, signups, activation (first inbox connected), free→paid conversion. — resolves: growth_lever_fits / pmf; why: stated channels (blog/SEO/content + your audience) are ones the buyer can't run; we need to see if anything else brings users.

Any cancellations or downgrades to date? Current logo churn since launch? — resolves: pmf_retention; why: 5 months is short, but early churn among network customers would confirm the revenue is courtesy, not demand.

Why sell now at 1.3x rather than run it to $1k MRR and sell at 3x? What does 'shifting focus' mean concretely for support/transition availability? — resolves: seller_adverse_selection; why: the insider's price is information.

Is the price negotiable toward ~$3,000, or would you consider seller financing / an earnout tied to MRR retention at 6 months? — resolves: deal_terms / affordability; why: asking is 1.67x the buyer's ceiling; an MRR-retention earnout also directly hedges the network-revenue risk.
inputs_provided: Listing URL trustmrr.com/startup/getbeel (user) — VERIFIED as existing

TrustMRR .md profile figures: $660 all-time, $331 last-30d, $333 MRR, +53.4%/30d, 5 active subs, 85% margin, DR 9.0, $5,000 asking, 1.3x, 176 viewers, 2 offers, founded 2026-02-14, Italy, channels blog/content/SEO — MARKETPLACE_ATTESTED

Product details from getbeel.com: Gmail/Outlook/IMAP invoice extraction, bank reconciliation, Stripe/QuickBooks/Xero integrations, EN/IT/ES/FR, showcase customers VideoTok/TaxCoach/Mimoso/Editby/Filmia — VERIFIED (live site)
Founder identity: Borja Soler (Reyes), The Formula AI (Milan) — builds VideoTok, Editby, Filmia, Getbeel; 3 of 5 showcase customers are his own products — VERIFIED (public sources)
Competitive set: Dext, Hubdoc, AutoEntry, Datamolino, GetMyInvoices + indie clones (Tailride, Taxing.app, Gennai) — VERIFIED (public sources)
Budget ceiling $3,000 / no financing — reused from session convention

Borja Soler Reyes — personal site (The Formula AI: Editby, Videotok, Filmia, Getbeel)

scorecard_markdown:
# Acquisition Scorecard (v2) — Getbeel (getbeel.com)

**Type:** micro-SaaS / B2B tool (AI invoice collection & reconciliation)  ·  **Asking:** $5,000 (1.3x annualized MRR)  ·  **Budget:** $3,000  ·  **Date:** 2026-07-18
**Figures reconciled?** two-source ✓ (listing .md + live site) · verify_figures ✓ (subs×ARPU≈MRR ✓, asking/ARR≈1.25x ✓) · revenue evidence: marketplace_attested
**Sources reviewed:** TrustMRR .md profile, getbeel.com, founder's public footprint (Medium/PH/personal site), competitor landscape (Dext, Hubdoc, AutoEntry, indie clones)

## Verdict

> **PASS (provisional) — 49/100, OUT OF BUDGET** (completeness 54%)
> The strongest raw material seen from TrustMRR so far — genuinely recurring B2B revenue ($333 MRR, 5 subs at $29–79) in a category with a real EU e-invoicing regulatory tailwind — but three things stop it. (1) **Growth-lever gate (verified): capped at PASS** — stated channels are blog/content/SEO plus the founder's indie-hacker audience, exactly the marketing treadmill the team won't run. (2) **The revenue may not be arm's-length:** 3 of 5 showcase customers (VideoTok, Editby, Filmia) are the founder's own products; if the paying subs overlap, real external MRR could be ~$100–150 and churn on transfer. (3) **Affordability: $5,000 is 1.67x the $3,000 ceiling, no financing, and 2 competing offers already in.** Biggest swing factor: the subscriber list — arm's-length subs + a price near $3k would re-rate this toward MARGINAL/PROMISING territory; network subs confirm PASS at any price.

## Scorecard (nine dimensions)

| # | Dimension | Wt | Score /5 | Conf | Weighted | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Operability & product upside | 15 | 3 | med | 9.0 | Exact stack fit; real upside (integrations, more countries). Drag: Italian tax compliance + Italian-language accountant support are outside the team's domain. |
| 2 | PMF & retention | 15 | 2 | low | 6.0 | Real B2B price points and +53%/30d, but 5 subs, no retention history, and the subs may be the founder's network. |
| 3 | Unit economics & upside | 12 | 3 | low | 7.2 | ~$66 ARPU recurring, 85% margin claimed; structurally sound if customers are real. |
| 4 | Moat & defensibility | 12 | 2 | med | 4.8 | Dext/Hubdoc/AutoEntry + a wave of indie clones; LLMs commoditize extraction. Italy angle modest; Italian incumbents own that niche. |
| 5 | Growth & market | 8 | 3 | med | 4.8 | EU e-invoicing mandates are a genuine tailwind; AI cuts both ways. |
| 6 | Tech & IP health | 8 | 3 | low | 4.8 | Modern stack; verify Google restricted-scope OAuth (CASA) transfer + Italian SDI intermediary contract. |
| 7 | Transfer / key-person | 10 | 2 | med | 4.0 | PH launches, founder's audience, studio cross-promotion — much of the demand engine is personal. |
| 8 | Deal terms & value | 10 | 2 | high | 4.0 | Below-market multiple on paper, but $5k for $660 all-time revenue, 1.67x budget, 2 rival offers. |
| 9 | Seller & adverse-selection | 10 | 2 | med | 4.0 | Credible builder-churn, but insider sells a growing asset at 1.3x with his own logos on the homepage — the price is information. |
| | **Merit total** | **100** | | | **48.6** | |

**Gates:** operable ✓ · growth-lever-fits ✗ (caps → PASS) · revenue-evidence marketplace_attested (provisional) · value-transfers ? · IP-transferable ? · not-fragile ? · **affordable** NO ($5,000 vs $3,000, no financing → OUT OF BUDGET)

## Fit-to-build read (the team's lens)

Half of this deal fits beautifully: the stack is the team's exact stack, and the roadmap (more integrations, more countries' e-invoicing formats, better bank matching) is pure engineering. The other half doesn't: customers arrive via content marketing, SEO, and the founder's personal indie-hacker audience — none of which transfer or fit the team — and the Italy-specific compliance features need Italian domain knowledge and support. Buying it means inheriting a content-marketing job plus a compliance beat, with the engineering already mostly done.

## Does the value transfer? (key-person check)

This is the crux. The founder runs a four-product studio, his own products are the showcase customers, and his audience is the distribution. What verifiably transfers: code, domain (DR 9), Stripe billing, and whichever subscribers are arm's-length — which is exactly the unverified part. An MRR-retention earnout would price this risk correctly if the deal were pursued.

## What we know vs. what we're assuming

- **Verified (buyer-reconciled):** product is live and substantial (Gmail/Outlook/IMAP extraction, bank matching, QBO/Xero integrations, 4 languages); founder identity and studio; 3 of 5 showcase logos are the founder's own products; figures internally consistent; competitive field crowded and commoditizing.
- **Assumed / marketplace-attested:** all dollar figures (Stripe-attested badge); 85% margin; +53%/30d growth; 'blog/content/SEO' as the channel.
- **Unknown (and how it swings):** subscriber identities (the deal-decider); Google OAuth restricted-scope verification transfer; SDI intermediary contract; churn to date; price flexibility. Best realistic case (all subs arm's-length, OAuth transfers, price ≤$3,750) ≈ MARGINAL~55, provisional; worst case confirms PASS.

## Seller questions to send (prioritized)

1. List the 5 active subscriptions (company or industry + plan + start date) and confirm none are owned by you, The Formula AI, or affiliates; share read-only Stripe access. — *resolves: value_transfers / pmf / revenue → buyer_verified; your showcase logos are your own products.*
2. Google OAuth status for Gmail restricted scopes — verified / in review / <100-user exemption? Does the verified Cloud project transfer, and what did CASA cost? — *resolves: ip_transferable / not_fragile.*
3. Which SDI / fattura elettronica intermediary, what contract/cost, does it transfer, and how much Italy compliance maintenance so far? — *resolves: tech_ip / operability.*
4. 90-day funnel: visitors by source, signups, activation, free→paid. — *resolves: growth_lever / pmf.*
5. Cancellations or downgrades to date? — *resolves: retention.*
6. Why sell a growing asset at 1.3x now instead of at $1k MRR for 3x? — *resolves: adverse-selection.*
7. Would you take ~$3,000, or seller financing / an earnout tied to 6-month MRR retention? — *resolves: affordability + hedges the network-revenue risk.*

## Recommended next step

**Pass at $5,000; worth one email if curious.** This is the first TrustMRR target where the seller questions could actually change the answer — but two of the three blockers (channel mismatch, budget) survive even perfect answers. Only re-engage if (a) the subscriber list proves arm's-length external revenue, and (b) the price drops to ≤$3,000–3,750 or the seller takes an MRR-retention earnout. Otherwise the corpus pattern holds: a serial builder's 5-month-old product, dressed by a marketplace multiple, whose growth engine leaves with him.
